snp5.13:1.1: I entreat the one who has abandoned home—cutting through craving, unperturbed;
snp5.13:1.3: abandoned delight—crossed over the flood, released;
snp5.13:1.5: Having heard the Nāga, they will leave—
snp5.13:2.1: the many gathered from many lands, hero,
snp5.13:2.4:
how this Dhamma has been known to you.
The Buddha:
snp5.13:3.4: For whatever people cling to in the world,
snp5.13:3.5: it’s through that that Māra pursues them.
snp5.13:4.1:
So a monk, mindful, seeing these people entangled in grasping as entangled in Death’s realm,
should cling to nothing in all the world, every world.
